stimulus: How do paleobotanists estimate which plant communities surrounded ancient lake basins? Historically, researchers were confined to identifying fossilized leaves and seeds, but in 2018 a group led by Diego Mora of the Universidad Autónoma de Madrid tried something ______blank the pollen DNA trapped inside layered lakebed sediments.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "A group led by Diego Mora...tried something different" is a complete independent clause that promises an elaboration of what "different" thing was tried. A colon correctly introduces that elaboration.

Choice A is incorrect because "Extracting...sediments" has no subject and cannot stand alone as a sentence. Choice C is incorrect because a semicolon can only join two independent clauses, and "extracting...sediments" is not independent. Choice D is incorrect because the colon is misplaced; a colon must end an independent clause, not appear after the elaboration begins.
